symptoms.symptom_name: Persistent heat intolerance with drenching sweats

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hypermetabolism raises heat production — a hallmark of thyrotoxicosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Unintentional weight loss ~12 kg with increased appetite

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Catabolic weight loss despite increased intake is characteristic of a hypermetabolic thyrotoxic state.
Weight loss can occur but is less prominent and lacks the eye/neck signs.

symptoms.symptom_name: Fine resting tremor of the hands

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Enhanced beta-adrenergic tone produces a fine postural/resting tremor.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sustained resting palpitations (~130-135 bpm reported on wearable)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sinus tachycardia at rest is one of the most consistent cardiovascular features of thyrotoxicosis.
A sustained tachycardia at ~135 mandates ECG assessment for an unstable rhythm or ischaemia, which telehealth cannot provide.

symptoms.symptom_name: Profound proximal muscle weakness — unable to rise from a chair

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Thyrotoxic proximal myopathy produces exactly this pattern and signals severity.
Thyroid eye disease (exophthalmos/lid retraction) is specific to Graves' among causes of thyrotoxicosis; the lay report is a proxy for the sealed exam finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Thickened plaques on the shins (lay report of pretibial skin change)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Lay proxy for pretibial myxoedema, a Graves-specific dermopathy; the characterised sign is a sealed examination finding (SYM-012).

symptoms.symptom_name: Diffuse smooth goitre with a thyroid bruit

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A diffuse goitre with an audible bruit reflects hypervascular autoimmune thyroid overactivity — clinician auscultation/palpation, not obtainable via telehealth.
Formal ocular examination confirming exophthalmos and lid lag is highly specific for Graves' — a sealed clinician finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Brisk (3+) globally exaggerated deep tendon reflexes

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hyperreflexia reflects the hyperadrenergic state — a sealed neurological examination finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sinus tachycardia at 135 bpm with widened pulse pressure and bounding pulses

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective hyperdynamic circulation — the clinician measurement; the AI infers a hyperadrenergic state from the patient-reported wearable HR, not a measured pulse character.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pretibial myxoedema (non-pitting thickened plaques characterised on exam)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pretibial myxoedema is a Graves-specific dermopathy; formal characterisation is a sealed examination finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Suppressed TSH with elevated free T4/T3 (anticipated on urgent labs)

investigation_details.investigation_name: Thyroid function tests (TSH, free T4, free T3)
investigation_details.expected_result: Suppressed TSH with elevated free T4 and/or free T3 (biochemical thyrotoxicosis)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Diagnosis remains unconfirmed and antithyroid therapy cannot be safely initiated.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Biochemical confirmation of thyrotoxicosis; not obtainable via telehealth in real time, so the AI must arrange it, never assert a value.

symptoms.symptom_name: No high fever, confusion, chest pain or breathlessness at rest at time of contact

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Their absence means storm has not yet declared, but does NOT downgrade urgency — these are the tipping-point features to safety-net for.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The co-occurrence of a diffuse goitre with bruit, thyroid eye disease and pretibial myxoedema on a backdrop of hyperadrenergic thyrotoxicosis is effectively pathognomonic of Graves'.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assess for tachyarrhythmia (e.g. atrial fibrillation) or ischaemia driving/complicating the sustained tachycardia — cannot be done via telehealth, so escalate.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Thyroid function tests (TSH, free T4, free T3)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirm biochemical thyrotoxicosis; suppressed TSH with elevated free T4/T3 is the diagnostic result.
Confirms Graves' aetiology and guides definitive therapy.
Baseline before antithyroid therapy (agranulocytosis and hepatotoxicity risk) and to assess the systemic impact of severe thyrotoxicosis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Empirical outpatient thyroid ultrasound before escalation
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Imaging must not delay emergency assessment and rate control in a patient with impending thyroid storm.
